title: Conversion Table for Percentage Scores, Letter Grades, and Grade Points

content.A+_A: Academic Excellence. Full and creative understanding of the course content at the highest level.
content.B+_B: Above Average Achievement. Good understanding of the course content, with skills to apply concepts.
content.C+_C: Average Achievement. Satisfactory understanding of the course content, prepared for further study.
content.D: Below Average. Concepts not consistently understood; lacks sufficient knowledge, skills, and attitude.
content.F: Inadequate. Failed to understand course concepts; knowledge, skills, and attitude are insufficient.

title: 4.5 Evaluation of Student Knowledge, Skills, and Attitude

content.description: The evaluation of knowledge, skills, and attitudes acquired by the learner shall be guided by the principle of encouraging and developing independent learning activities. Evaluation must be scientifically based, objective, fair, and transparent.
Formula for calculating Grade Point Average (GPA): GPA = Σ(Kᵢ × Oᵢ) / ΣKᵢ. Where Kᵢ = credit hours of the course, Oᵢ = score/grade of the course. Calculated with one decimal place precision.

The knowledge, skills, and attitude acquired by the learner in a given course shall be evaluated out of 100 points, factoring in both continuous assessment and final examination results accordingly (per Appendix 1).
A score of 60 or above is considered successful and earns credits. A score below 60 is considered 'inadequate' (F) and counts as failing to fulfill the credit requirement.
Comprehensive exams and research work are evaluated out of 100 points. A score of 60+ results in 'Satisfactory' (S); a score below 60 results in 'Unsatisfactory' (U).
Instructors are required to enter course grades into the learning management system within 5 working days after the date of the final exam.
Failure to enter course grades within the specified period will result in liability according to the 'NUM Internal Labor Regulations'.
The 4 credits of research work for master's students and 12 credits for doctoral students shall be calculated in accordance with clause 4.3.14.
Students who receive a failing grade in a core course, or those with R, W, WF, or U evaluations, must retake the course to fulfill the credit requirement.

A student may retake a course they have already passed (assigned an R grade). The number of courses a student can retake during their period of study is limited to 3. Dual degree students may retake up to 3 courses per program.
The use of the W grade (withdrawal from a course) is prohibited more than 6 times during the period of study.

Students who violate examination rules (cheating, impersonation, using unauthorized materials, etc.) will receive an F grade and be cited for an ethical violation. Repeated offenses will be discussed by the School Administration Board or the NUM Ethics Committee.

If a student has a grievance regarding a grade, they must contact the instructor within the first 14 days of the following semester. If unresolved, a complaint can be filed with the Academic Affairs unit.

title: Special Letter Notations and Evaluation Descriptions

content.description: A request to withdraw from the final exam after completing at least 2/3 of the course content with a continuous assessment score of 60%+. Does not affect GPA.

(Withdrawal Fail) A notation assigned by the instructor 7 days before the end of the semester if a student has missed more than 1/3 of any form of the course activities. This notation cannot be assigned after the deadline has passed.
Assigned when continuous assessment is incomplete or the final exam was missed due to valid excuses. Must be completed within the first 14 days of the next semester.
Inadequate. Failed to understand course concepts; knowledge, skills, and attitude are insufficient. Calculated as 0 in GPA.
Automatically assigned by the system if the instructor fails to submit grades on time. It will automatically convert to the corresponding letter grade once the appeal period ends.
Assigned by the system in cases of leave for military service, quarantine, emergencies, or surgery.
Assigned when a request is made to retake a successfully completed course for the purpose of improving the grade.

title: Methodology for Evaluating Student Knowledge, Skills, and Attitude
formula: Total Score (O) = Continuous Assessment (O1) + Semester Final Exam (O2)

details.O1_progress: Continuous Assessment (70-80 points): The instructor evaluates the semester by dividing it into at least 3 parts. Forms include: participation, Q&A, seminars, laboratories, independent assignments, reports, case studies, and mid-term exams.
details.precision: Evaluations shall be accurate to the nearest whole number.
